authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(2) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland streams, rivers, and canals)
sources.quotedText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions. From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish. In waters where the bag limit for trout is zero, trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Cascade Creek not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Cascade Creek not listed by name)
CCR T14 §7.50(b)(152) — Tahoe Lake and tributaries special regulation (extent: 'upstream to the first lake')
sources.quotedText: (152) Tahoe Lake and tributaries (Placer and El Dorado Cos.). (A) Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

end.verbatim: the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April

parameters.note: Cascade Creek is a Lake Tahoe tributary (it flows NE from the Desolation Wilderness lakes into Cascade Lake, whose outlet then reaches Lake Tahoe near Emerald Bay), BUT the Tahoe tributary special regulation §7.50(b)(152)(A) applies only to 'Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake' — i.e. from Lake Tahoe up to Cascade Lake (the first lake). The documented reach (lon/lat ~38.927, -120.106) lies ABOVE Cascade Lake, so the special tributary closure does NOT reach it; Cascade Creek is not listed by name in §7.40(b) or §7.50(b) either. Therefore the general statewide trout stream regulation §5.85(a)(2) governs this reach: open last Saturday in April through November 15 (5 trout/day, 10 possession, no gear restrictions), then a zero-trout artificial-barbless-only period November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April.

regulations.humanSummary: General trout stream season: open last Saturday in April through November 15; zero-trout catch-and-release (artificial barbless only) November 16 through the Friday before the last Saturday in April. (Tahoe tributary special reg §7.50(b)(152)(A) stops at the first lake — Cascade Lake — and does not reach this above-lake segment.)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions. From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland streams during the open season (last Saturday in April through November 15): five-trout daily bag and ten-trout possession. From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April a zero-trout bag limit applies (§5.85(a)(2)).

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit during the open season (zero-trout Nov 16 through the Friday before the last Saturday in April).
regulations.verbatimText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions.

parameters.note: Gear restriction applies only during the winter zero-trout period; during the open season (last Saturday in April–November 15) there are no gear restrictions and bait is allowed.

regulations.humanSummary: November 16 through the Friday before the last Saturday in April: zero-trout, artificial lures with barbless hooks only. No gear restrictions during the open season.
regulations.verbatimText: From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. Cascade Creek is wholly within California (El Dorado Co.); no Nevada reciprocity applies.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+.
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
